header.title: Comment ça marche — flux de données complet

sections.title: NewsAPI Ingester
sections.summary: Pulls articles from NewsAPI for one or more topics, deduplicates by sha256, and merges tags when an article matches multiple topics.

annotations.5: Dedup by sha256(title+source) — re-ingesting the same article produces the same id, upsert is idempotent.
annotations.10: Tag fusion: an article retrieved via both Python and AI/ML topics inherits both tags.
annotations.18: page_size=100 is the NewsAPI free-tier maximum.
annotations.21: Truncate dates to YYYY-MM-DD — NewsAPI rejects timezone-aware values.

sections.title: HTML Scraping & Cleaning
sections.summary: NewsAPI returns ~200 chars per article. The scraper fetches the full HTML, strips boilerplate, and converts the result to Markdown.

annotations.3: timeout=10s — if the source is slow, fall back to NewsAPI's 200-char description.
annotations.7: User-Agent set: many sites block default Python agents.
annotations.12: raise_for_status() lets the caller treat 4xx/5xx uniformly.
annotations.18: lxml is C-backed, ~4× faster than the stdlib parser.
annotations.22: strip_boilerplate removes 30–60% of HTML weight before Markdown conversion.

sections.title: Embedding & Indexation Pipeline
sections.summary: Splits scraped articles into 1200-char chunks with 200-char overlap, embeds them in batches, and upserts into ChromaDB.

annotations.5: 1200 chars (~200 words) per chunk — long enough for meaning, short enough for precision.
annotations.6: 200-char overlap stitches meaning across chunk boundaries.
annotations.16: Deterministic sha256 → re-ingest is a safe no-op (upsert handles duplicates).
annotations.21: Batches of 16 keep the embedding API within request limits.
annotations.26: documents stored raw inside ChromaDB so we can display them in cards without a separate store.

sections.title: ChromaDB client & cosine space
sections.summary: Single shared HNSW collection configured with cosine similarity — the architectural decision that drives every downstream distance computation.

annotations.1: lru_cache(maxsize=1): one HTTP client for the whole process lifetime.
annotations.11: hnsw:space='cosine' — without this, ChromaDB defaults to L2 (Euclidean), which behaves differently for variable-length text.
annotations.15: HNSW is a logarithmic-time graph index — fast lookup across millions of vectors.

sections.title: Chat orchestration
sections.summary: handle_chat() runs five sequential steps and is the only entry point the API surface calls.

annotations.3: Query expansion combines the question with selected topics.
annotations.8: k=8 — always returns 8 chunks even when relevance is low (filtering is the LLM's job).
annotations.12: Enrichment retrieves the full article for each retrieved chunk's parent.
annotations.17: Fresh news bypasses embedding — fetched live from NewsAPI.

sections.title: Vector search (RAG)
sections.summary: Embeds the expanded query, asks ChromaDB for the 8 nearest neighbours by cosine distance, and returns content + metadata + distance.

annotations.4: embed() — Azure AI Inference, same model used at ingest time. Symmetry matters.
annotations.7: n_results=k — ChromaDB always returns k, distances tell you whether to trust them.
annotations.9: Distances must be requested explicitly — they're not in the default include set.
annotations.16: Truncate to 2000 chars before embedding — avoid the model's token cap silently.

sections.title: Chunk enrichment
sections.summary: Reconstructs the full article for the top 5 retrieved chunks by pulling every sibling chunk back from ChromaDB.

annotations.6: Keep only the closest chunk per document_id — avoid retrieving the same article via multiple chunks.
annotations.10: Top 5 only — context budget is finite; the rest stays as raw chunks.
annotations.16: collection.get() (not query) — no semantic ranking needed, we already know which doc we want.
annotations.21: Sort by chunk_index to rebuild the article in original order.
annotations.25: distance=0.0 marks the enriched article as max-priority for the LLM.

sections.title: LLM synthesis
sections.summary: Builds the system + user payload, calls Azure AI Inference with JSON-strict output, returns Markdown.

annotations.5: JSON-strict output: the LLM is forced into a parseable shape — no free-text drift.
annotations.9: lru_cache(maxsize=1) — share the HTTP client, reuse the TLS handshake.
annotations.13: temperature=0.2 — stable, reproducible answers. 1.0 would be creative but unreliable.
annotations.17: max_completion_tokens=4096 — caps Mistral-Large-3 output to avoid silent truncation.
annotations.24: ainvoke is async — FastAPI's event loop stays free during LLM latency.
annotations.29: Cards reference ids the LLM cites; the UI resolves them to clickable sources.

sections.title: Card construction
sections.summary: Turns retrieved chunks and fresh articles into ArticleCard objects, mapping distance to a 0–100 similarity score for the UI.

annotations.6: snippet[:280] — short preview; the LLM saw [:600] in its context.
annotations.8: card_type='rag' lets the frontend show the SimilarityBar for these cards only.
annotations.9: distance ∈ [0, 2] — ChromaDB with cosine returns 1 - cosine_similarity.
annotations.15: Fresh news has no distance — they didn't pass through the embedding model.
annotations.22: json.loads then fallback to raw — be lenient if the LLM ever skips the JSON envelope.
